manufacturer.comment: Manufactured under license; broadly distributed by Digi-Key, Newark, Mouser, Element14.

release-date.comment: Announced and made orderable in March 2023.

status.comment: Still listed at $99 USD via Digi-Key, Newark, Mouser, Element14 as of 2026-04-26.

architecture-family.comment: Quad Cortex-A53 application cores are ARMv8 (aarch64); on-die Cortex-M4F and Cortex-R5F MCUs are auxiliary.

open-source-hardware.comment: BeagleBoard.org publishes full design files (schematics, layout) on OpenBeagle, consistent with the BeagleBoard.org open-hardware tradition. No matching OSHWA UID surfaced in this pass — certification status unverified.

country-of-origin.comment: BeagleBoard.org Foundation is headquartered in Michigan, USA; PCBs assembled in China.

soc.comment: Specifically the AM6254 SKU within TI's AM62x Sitara family.

soc-vendor.comment: TI (Texas Instruments) — `ti` tag not yet defined in attributes.json; using `other` per ongoing schema gap (same workaround as beaglebone-black/beaglebone-ai-64). User has flagged for batch schema fix.

cpu-cores.comment: 4× Cortex-A53 application cores. Cortex-M4F MCU and PRU subsystem not counted as application CPU cores.

cpu-architecture.value: 4× Cortex-A53 (+ 1× Cortex-M4F MCU @ 400 MHz, 2× PRU @ 333 MHz)

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Cortex-A53 application cores at up to 1.4 GHz.

process-node.comment: AM625 is fabricated on a 16 nm FinFET process per TI's product page.

gpu.comment: Imagination PowerVR Rogue AXE-1-16M; vendor cites >500 Mpixel/s fillrate, >8 GFLOPS.

vulkan-support.comment: Vulkan 1.2 supported by the AXE-1-16M. As of Feb 2026, fully upstream PowerVR DRM kernel driver + Mesa Vulkan driver landed for BeaglePlay — open-source Vulkan 1.2 acceleration without proprietary blobs.

npu.comment: AM625 has no dedicated NPU/MMA. TI markets the device as 'edge AI enabled' but inference relies on the Cortex-A53 cluster (and optionally PRU/M4F).

hw-video-decode.comment: AM625 is an HMI-class SoC with no dedicated hardware video codec block. Decode happens on the A53 cluster (software). Empty tag set indicates no hardware codec acceleration.

hw-video-encode.comment: No hardware video encoder on AM625.

ram-min-gb.comment: Single 2 GB SKU; no other RAM options sold.

ram-type.comment: Vendor design docs and CNX coverage both list DDR4 (not LPDDR4); the early launch coverage's 'DDR2' mention is a typo.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: No M.2 connector of any key/length on the board.

ethernet.comment: Gigabit Ethernet via RJ45 (RTL8211F PHY). Highest port speed; the second SPE/T1L port is 10 Mbps.

dual-ethernet.comment: Two physical Ethernet ports: Gigabit RJ45 + 10 Mbps single-pair Ethernet (10Base-T1L) RJ11 with power-over-data-line. SPE port is much slower than the gigabit one.

wifi.comment: TI WL1807MOD (WiLink 8) provides 2.4/5 GHz dual-band Wi-Fi 4 (802.11n only); no Wi-Fi 5/6 support.

bluetooth.comment: TI CC1352P7 wireless MCU provides Bluetooth 5.2 LE plus 802.15.4 / Sub-GHz radios.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: AM625 USB subsystem is USB 2.0 only — the single USB-A host port is USB 2.0 (480 Mbps), not 5 Gbps.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB-C carrying USB 2.0 data and power input.

poe-support.comment: No standard PoE on the gigabit port. The SPE/T1L port carries power-over-data-line (10Base-T1L PoDL) but that is a separate, low-power, single-pair industrial standard, not 802.3af/at/bt PoE.

max-display-resolution.comment: AM625 display subsystem caps at 1920×1080 @ 60 fps per output. No 4K support on this SoC.

displayport.comment: No native DisplayPort. The OLDI (LVDS) FPC connector for touchscreens is not DisplayPort. USB-C is USB 2.0 only (no DP-Alt).

mipi-dsi.comment: Display FPC is OLDI/LVDS (4-lane), not MIPI-DSI. AM625 itself has no MIPI-DSI controller.

mipi-csi.comment: 22-pin 4-lane MIPI-CSI camera FPC connector, compatible with Raspberry Pi camera modules.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm analog audio jack. Audio routes via HDMI or external add-ons (mikroBUS/Grove/QWIIC).

gpio-pins.comment: BeaglePlay deliberately drops the BeagleBone P8/P9 cape headers and the RPi 40-pin header. Expansion is via 1× mikroBUS, 1× Grove, 1× QWIIC, plus camera/display FPCs. Recording 0 to reflect the absence of a generic GPIO pin header; raw GPIOs are still reachable through the expansion connectors.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: No RPi HAT compatibility — neither header pinout nor mounting-hole pattern matches.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: AM625 has no PCIe controller, so no PCIe lanes are exposed on BeaglePlay.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C (QWIIC + Grove + mikroBUS), SPI (mikroBUS), UART (mikroBUS + console) all exposed via the expansion connectors.

power-input.comment: USB-C only (no barrel jack). Vendor recommends 5V ≥ 3A (15 W) supply.

idle-power-w.comment: No reliable independent idle-power measurement surfaced for BeaglePlay (Phoronix/Geerling/CNX-Software did not publish a measured number). 15 W USB-C recommendation is a PSU headroom figure, not idle draw.

load-power-w.comment: No reliable independent full-load measurement surfaced. Vendor PSU recommendation of 15 W is a maximum allowance.

cooling-required.comment: Cortex-A53 quad @ 1.4 GHz on 16 nm with no NPU and no GPU video block runs cool; vendor neither bundles a heatsink nor mandates active cooling. KKSB metal case acts as a passive heatspreader.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC chip with battery holder for timekeeping when unpowered.

length-mm.comment: PCB is 80 × 80 mm (vendor lists overall product as 82.5 × 80 × 20 mm including overhang/connectors).

form-factor-class.comment: 80×80 mm square — neither pi-standard (~85×56), pi-zero, nor any ITX size. Effectively a custom BeaglePlay-specific footprint.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Custom mounting-hole pattern to match KKSB and BeagleBoard.org-blessed cases; not RPi-standard.

weight-g.comment: Bare board weight per vendor design doc.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Mainline U-Boot has board support (am62x_beagleplay). As of Feb 2026, the PowerVR DRM kernel driver and Mesa Vulkan driver are upstream — closing the last major peripheral gap. Wi-Fi (WL1807) and BT (CC1352P7) work via standard upstream drivers. Treating as 'full' on that basis; some users still prefer TI's vendor 6.12/6.18 kernel for the most polished out-of-the-box experience.

official-os-image.comment: BeagleBoard.org publishes maintained Debian images (XFCE and minimal) across kernel 6.12.x, 6.18.x, and 6.19.x lines.

distros-with-images.comment: Debian (official BeagleBoard images) plus Ubuntu 26.04, Debian 14 Forky, Kali, Home Assistant, OpenMediaVault, openHAB via Armbian's BeaglePlay page (10 images, vendor 6.12.57 / 6.18.13 kernels).

bootloader.comment: Mainline U-Boot has am62x_beagleplay board support; TI ROM bootloader handles initial stages.

long-term-support.comment: BeagleBoard.org has historically maintained images for many years (BeagleBone Black is still supported >10 years on), but no formally committed LTS window is published for BeaglePlay.

android-support.comment: TI publishes a Processor SDK Android for AM62x and there are community forum threads about Android-13 on BeaglePlay, but no first-class maintained, regularly-updated Android image is shipped on the BeagleBoard distros page. Marking false on the 'official or maintained' bar.

msrp-base-usd.comment: $99 USD launch MSRP held since March 2023 across Digi-Key/Newark/Mouser/Element14 listings as of 2026-04-26. Single configuration, so base = top.

available-regions.comment: Distributed via Digi-Key, Newark, Mouser, Element14, OKdo and similar — global retail footprint.

typical-lead-time.comment: Listed as available at Newark and Digi-Key as of 2026-04-26; no scarcity flags surfaced this pass.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive multi-chapter docs site (introduction, quick-start, design, expansion, demos), schematics, kernel/U-Boot how-tos. One notch below RPi/Hardkernel due to occasional gaps and SDK fragmentation between TI vendor stack and BeagleBoard images.

community-size.comment: BeagleBoard ecosystem is broadly large/established; BeaglePlay specifically has narrower mindshare than RPi but benefits from the shared forum, docs, and Bootlin training material.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run Discourse forum (forum.beagleboard.org) with regular activity, including BeaglePlay-specific threads.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: BeaglePlay drops cape compatibility, so it relies on mikroBUS Click boards (huge MikroElektronika library), Grove modules, QWIIC modules, plus a few BeaglePlay-specific cases (KKSB). Solid but not RPi-scale; no HAT ecosystem.
